inline_comments.message:
shouldn't this be somehow calculated from the flavor itself?

If it is left as is then when someone changes the "small" flavor for his environment or define other flavor "super small" then this is not going to work right?

I thought about that but it's not super simple to say the least.
What I can do is to allow to change also and to explain roughly how to make it work
this looks better but how about tight it to the amount of RAM of active flavor rather than to the flavor name itself?

because we don't need it if RAM > 8G
and new_heap is roughly tied to cpus.
to be honest, I think that if someone wants to change the values, he has to read how to do it because it's not easy to automate (in particular because of metrics unit abbrev are different) as we're quite limited with go template language.

So, we have 3 cases:
* no limits so MAX_HEAP_SIZE, HEAP_NEW_SIZE won't be set. As we certainly have more than 8gb, it's not an issue
* "big" flavor, MAX_HEAP_SIZE, HEAP_NEW_SIZE won't be set. As we certainly have more than 8gb, it's not an issue
* "small" flavor is used, MAX_HEAP_SIZE, HEAP_NEW_SIZE will be set. As we certainly have less than 8gb, it's not an issue. Even if it's more than 8G, you can set the values accordingly.

If someone wants to be smart and use "specific" values with "big" flavor but less than 8Gb, too bad for him (it's the only case where we can have troubles)
OK fair enough.. We can always improve this in a future if people complain about it
